mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that immediately triggers equity sell-offs as investors price in reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings declines.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month out-of-sample period despite both series trending upward.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.0 percent quarter-over-quarter during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity utilization peaks that precede profit margin compression and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of late-cycle periods when industrial production growth exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product reflects sustained economic expansion that immediately boosts equity valuations through higher expected cash flows and risk appetite over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates a negative cumulative return over any 12-month rolling window or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark by 0.5 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index year-over-year growth surpassing 2.5 percent forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, which raises discount rates and triggers immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets record positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months following CPI spikes above the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that compresses wage margins and produces negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no predictive power with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests over a 3-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during post-shock recovery phases signal fragile consumer confidence that prevents equity markets from sustaining rallies, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns exceed zero in more than 65 percent of post-shock recovery months when retail sales decline.
